summary: cautilus version --verbose is the quickest binary-only summary of the product surface
anchor: is also the quickest binary-only summary of what

summary: Legacy Homebrew installs are not a supported update channel
anchor: Legacy Homebrew installs are not a supported update channel anymore

summary: Starter kits under examples/starters provide closest-surface alternatives to init adapter
anchor: start from the closest starter kit under

summary: Each starter ships a pre-filled adapter, a canonical input fixture, and a README
anchor: Each starter ships a pre-filled `cautilus-adapter.yaml`, a canonical input fixture

summary: Starter placeholders are bootstrap help, not proof of a wired consumer workflow
anchor: Treat starter placeholders as bootstrap help, not as proof

summary: A minimal adapter must wire at least one real runnable path to reach doctor ready
anchor: must wire at least one real runnable path to reach `doctor ready`

summary: codex-home-mode isolated keeps user config and session state out of the eval
anchor: keeps user config and session state out of the eval

summary: codex-auth-mode inherit copies only Codex auth into the isolated home
anchor: copies only Codex auth into the isolated home

summary: Runner reports runner_auth_missing before spending the full live run on unauthenticatable cases
anchor: reports `runner_auth_missing` before spending the whole live run

summary: evaluate fixture chooses the runner whose surfaces contains the fixture surface key
anchor: chooses the runner whose `surfaces` contains the fixture surface key

summary: Fixture runtime reports fixture-smoke proof class even when adapter declares a stronger class
anchor: the observed proof class is `fixture-smoke` even when the adapter declares a stronger

summary: Non-fixture coding-agent runtime may report coding-agent-messaging class only with matching runtime telemetry
anchor: but only when the observed packet reports the matching runtime telemetry

summary: doctor binary reports binary health with no repo required
anchor: binary health, no repo required

summary: doctor commands provides command discovery safe for wrapper tooling and agent runtimes
anchor: command discovery, safe for wrapper tooling and agent runtimes

summary: doctor status provides no-input agent orientation over binary, adapter, agent-surface, and claim state
anchor: no-input agent orientation over binary, adapter, agent-surface, and claim state

summary: cautilus <subcommand> --help exits 0 for the registered native command surface
anchor: exits `0` for the registered native command surface

summary: doctor status --json emits cautilus.agent_status.v1, a read-only orientation packet
anchor: It emits `cautilus.agent_status.v1`: a read-only orientation packet

summary: next-action can point at runner assessment setup when runner proof is not ready
anchor: that next action can point at runner assessment setup

summary: doctor --scope agent-surface verifies only the Cautilus Agent and local agent-surface install
anchor: verify only the Cautilus Agent and local agent-surface install

summary: Default doctor --scope repo verifies the repo has a real runnable evaluation path
anchor: verify the repo has a real runnable evaluation path

summary: Each doctor check separates stable contract from run-specific observation
anchor: separates stable contract from run-specific observation

summary: doctor check fields id/ok/meaning/detail carry distinct readiness semantics
anchor: `id` names the machine-readable readiness condition, `ok` reports the boolean result

summary: External prerequisites record resolved binary path in detail when present and searched PATH when missing
anchor: put the resolved binary path in `detail` when present and the searched `PATH`

summary: Ready repo-scope doctor JSON includes first_bounded_run starter loop plus scenario catalog
anchor: the JSON payload includes `first_bounded_run`: a starter

summary: Named-adapter-only repos require --adapter-name for repo-scope validation
anchor: run `cautilus doctor --repo-root /path/to/repo --adapter-name <name>` for repo-scope validation

summary: Default discovery starts from adapter claim entries or README/AGENTS/CLAUDE and follows links to depth 3
anchor: follows repo-local Markdown links to depth 3

summary: refresh-plan packet includes refreshSummary with counts, hotspots, and next actions
anchor: which gives agent-readable counts, changed claim source hotspots

summary: Refresh-plan output is not a saved claim map update and must go to a refresh-plan artifact
anchor: Refresh-plan output is not a saved claim map update

summary: CLI rejects overwriting saved claim maps with refresh-plan packets due to schema mismatch
anchor: The CLI rejects that overwrite because refresh-plan packets use

summary: Normal discovery output is a proof plan rather than a verdict
anchor: it is a proof plan rather than a verdict

summary: discover claims status summarizes an existing claim packet without rescanning
anchor: to summarize an existing claim packet without rescanning

summary: When status gitState reports isStale=true, run refresh-plan before review or planning
anchor: when it reports `isStale=true`, run

summary: Status summary includes evidenceSatisfaction so satisfied claims are visible without full packet
anchor: so satisfied claims and their evidence refs are visible without reading the full claim packet

summary: gitState.isStale tracks recorded claim-source changes, not every HEAD change
anchor: tracks recorded claim-source changes, not every HEAD change

summary: Generated-artifact-only commits report headDrift and allow review or planning to continue
anchor: `gitState` reports `headDrift=true` with `comparisonStatus=fresh-with-head-drift`

summary: discover claims extraction-input emits deterministic agent-extraction input with hashes, template, hints, bounds
anchor: emit the deterministic agent-extraction input packet

summary: apply-extraction validates an agent result, anchors every excerpt, and composes the agent proof plan
anchor: validate an agent extraction result, anchor every verbatim excerpt

summary: Neither extraction command calls an LLM; invalid claims are rejected into rejectedClaims not silently dropped
anchor: Neither command calls an LLM; unanchored, unbounded, or out-of-scope claims are rejected

summary: review-input emits cautilus.claim_review_input.v1 and does not call an LLM or mark claims satisfied
anchor: does not call an LLM or mark claims satisfied

summary: Review input may include possibleEvidenceRefs as reviewer hints only
anchor: these are reviewer hints only

summary: Satisfied or already-reviewed claims are skipped by default and recorded under skippedClaims
anchor: are skipped by default and recorded under `skippedClaims`

summary: apply-review rejects evidenceStatus=satisfied unless a direct or verified evidence ref supports it
anchor: rejects `evidenceStatus=satisfied` unless a direct or verified evidence ref

summary: validate emits claim_validation_report.v1 and fails fast on invalid shape or evidence refs
anchor: fail fast on invalid packet shape or evidence refs

summary: validate does not mutate the packet or search for evidence
anchor: It does not mutate the packet or search for evidence

summary: evaluate claims plan turns ready cautilus-eval claims into claim_eval_plan.v1
anchor: to turn ready `cautilus-eval` claims into `cautilus.claim_eval_plan.v1`

summary: Satisfied claims are skipped by default in eval planning because they need no new fixture plan
anchor: are skipped by default because they do not need a new eval fixture plan

summary: Eval plan packet preserves claim ids, surfaces, refs, intents, questions but writes no host fixtures or runners
anchor: it deliberately does not write host-owned fixtures or runners

summary: review-input, apply-review, and eval claims plan reject stale claim packets by default
anchor: reject stale claim packets by default; `--allow-stale-claims` is an explicit operator override

summary: An eval live instance is one live consumer target selectable by stable id
anchor: is one live consumer target on this host that `Cautilus` can select by stable id

summary: discover live-targets resolves adapter instances or a probe command into one catalog packet
anchor: resolves either explicit adapter instances or a consumer-owned probe command into the same

summary: evaluate live takes one instance id plus one request packet and returns one bounded result packet
anchor: takes one selected instance id plus one request packet and returns one bounded result

summary: prepare-request-batch turns draft scenarios or a candidate packet into a canonical request batch
anchor: turns either `cautilus.scenario.v1` draft scenarios or a normalized catalog-candidate packet

summary: evaluate live scenarios schedules requests in-process and retries only when the batch asks and a prior attempt was transient
anchor: retries only when the batch packet asks for it and a prior attempt returned an explicit transient class

summary: The product owns the packet boundary and status semantics
anchor: The product owns the packet boundary and status semantics

summary: The consumer owns actual launch, auth, and runtime wiring through its adapter command
anchor: still owns actual launch, auth, and runtime wiring through its adapter command

summary: consumer_single_turn_command_template lets one command own a product-managed multi-turn chatbot loop
anchor: can also own a product-managed multi-turn chatbot loop above a consumer-owned single-turn seam

summary: The loop allocates a stable per-request workspace dir exposed via {workspace_dir}
anchor: allocates one stable per-request workspace directory at

summary: Cautilus runs workspace_prepare_command_template once before the first turn
anchor: runs that consumer-owned prepare command once before the first turn

summary: Persona prompt shaping and result semantics stay product-owned while backend selection stays adapter-owned
anchor: keeps persona prompt shaping and result semantics product-owned while backend selection stays adapter-owned

summary: consumer_evaluator_command_template materializes an evaluator input and expects a verdict back
anchor: materializes one `cautilus.live_run_evaluator_input.v1` packet and expects one

summary: The batch flow keeps prep and execution separate with distinct ownership of synthesis versus concurrency/retry
anchor: keeps prep and execution separate on purpose

summary: workspace start defaults --root to ./.cautilus/runs/ and prints export CAUTILUS_RUN_DIR
anchor: defaults `--root` to `./.cautilus/runs/` (auto-created on first use) and prints

summary: Passing --json yields the machine-readable payload instead of eval
anchor: Pass `--json` instead of `eval` when a script needs the machine-readable payload

summary: Downstream commands reuse a pinned CAUTILUS_RUN_DIR or auto-materialize a fresh runDir
anchor: downstream commands ... reuse that active `runDir`; if nothing is pinned they auto-materialize

summary: Normalize commands plus observation and report build accept --example-input printing a pipeable valid packet
anchor: it prints a minimal valid packet to stdout that can be piped back into the same command

summary: scenarios propose preserves the full ranked proposals list in canonical JSON output
anchor: now preserves the full ranked `proposals` list in the canonical JSON output

summary: The propose packet also emits attentionView, a bounded human-facing shortlist from the ranked set
anchor: which is a bounded human-facing shortlist derived from the full ranked set

summary: review-conversations stays intentionally narrower than a generic audit UI
anchor: stays intentionally narrower than a generic audit UI

summary: review-conversations links threads to proposals and coverage hints for behavior-eval review without browsing every turn
anchor: without browsing every live operator turn

summary: Cautilus exposes two top-level surfaces and four presets
anchor: Cautilus exposes two top-level surfaces and four presets

summary: The four presets are dev/repo, dev/skill, app/chat, and app/prompt with distinct purposes
anchor: `dev / repo` for repo work contracts, `dev / skill` for checked-in or portable development skills

summary: evaluate fixture runs a checked-in fixture through an adapter-owned runner then evaluates the observed packet
anchor: runs a checked-in fixture through an adapter-owned runner and then evaluates

summary: The command records a top-level proof object in observed and summary when proof class or assessment is identifiable
anchor: records a top-level `proof` object in `eval-observed.json` and `eval-summary.json`

summary: With runner_readiness.runners declared, the command selects the runner by fixture surface key before rendering
anchor: selects the runner by the fixture surface key before rendering the command template

summary: Fixture-runtime runs report fixture-smoke; adapter-declared proof class is preserved only as declared metadata
anchor: adapter-declared proof class is preserved only as declared metadata

summary: evaluate observation evaluates an already-observed packet without launching the runner again
anchor: evaluates an already-observed packet without launching the runner again

summary: The npm wrapper is the canonical maintainer-facing self-dogfood path for this repo
anchor: is the canonical maintainer-facing self-dogfood path for this repo

summary: Fixture variants prove the app/chat and app/prompt fixture translation, runner, and evaluator paths
anchor: prove the `app / chat` and `app / prompt` fixture translation, adapter runner, and evaluator packet paths

summary: Claude variants prove the app surface runner can use more than one provider backend
anchor: proving that the app surface runner can use more than one provider backend

summary: Backend variants are not eval live product-runner proof and report productProofReady=false until an app-owned runner assessment exists
anchor: are not `eval live` product-runner proof, and their current summaries report `productProofReady=false`

summary: skill-experiment emits skill_clone_experiment_report.v1 with delta, rubric match, coverage, isolation, recommendation
anchor: emits `cautilus.skill_clone_experiment_report.v1` with `variant_ran`, baseline-versus-variant delta

summary: skill-experiment does not install, mutate, or execute skills; host runners provide outputs
anchor: The command does not install, mutate, or execute skills

summary: --runtime fixture gives cheap command-routing proof over fixture-backed runner results
anchor: when the goal is cheap command-routing proof over fixture-backed runner results

summary: Fixture runtime is not evidence model-backed skill execution is good, only that the command surface lines up
anchor: It is not evidence that a model-backed skill execution is good

summary: report build answers what the first product-owned decision packet is for candidate versus baseline
anchor: what is the first product-owned decision packet for this candidate versus the

summary: evaluate review variants writes a review-summary plus one per-variant result file
anchor: writes a product-owned `review-summary.json` ... plus one per-variant

summary: A variant can finish passed, blocked, or failed; blocked runs carry machine-readable reason codes
anchor: blocked runs carry machine-readable reason codes instead of prose-only abort text

summary: Executor readiness failures like missing auth are classified blocked unavailable_executor not negative verdicts
anchor: classified as blocked `unavailable_executor` results rather than negative review verdicts

summary: When one variant passes and another does not, summary sets partialSuccess and lifts passing outputs
anchor: the summary sets `partialSuccess: true` and lifts the passing outputs

summary: With --output-under-test the review prompt switches to output_under_test mode treating it as primary evidence
anchor: switches into `output_under_test` mode and treats the referenced artifact as primary evidence

summary: With --output-text-key Cautilus extracts that JSON narrative span into the rendered prompt
anchor: extracts that JSON narrative span into the rendered prompt

summary: review feedback build writes cautilus.review_feedback.v1
anchor: `review feedback build` writes `cautilus.review_feedback.v1`

summary: review feedback build preserves a source review ref plus normalized method/proposal/disposition/note fields
anchor: preserves a source review reference plus normalized method, proposal, disposition, and note fields

summary: missing_critical may omit proposal evidence because it records a method missed a needed proposal
anchor: may omit proposal evidence because it records that the method missed a proposal

summary: Its disposition vocabulary is about review usefulness rather than pass/fail status
anchor: is about review usefulness rather than pass/fail status

summary: review feedback summarize writes cautilus.review_feedback_summary.v1
anchor: `review feedback summarize` writes `cautilus.review_feedback_summary.v1`

summary: review feedback summarize reads selected feedback packets and counts dispositions overall and by method family
anchor: counts review-useful dispositions overall and by method family

summary: review feedback summarize does not discover active-run defaults or treat dispositions as pass/fail
anchor: does not discover active-run defaults or treat dispositions as evaluator pass/fail status

summary: cautilus improve is the improvement front door
anchor: `cautilus improve` is the improvement front door

summary: improve should be used only after claim and eval proof surface is explicit
anchor: Use it only after the claim and eval proof surface are explicit enough

summary: Reports whose productRunnerProofReadiness is blocked cannot become improve inputs until product proof is ready
anchor: cannot become improve inputs until runner-backed product proof is ready

summary: Rendered HTML is read-only over the JSON files which remain the source of truth
anchor: The rendered HTML is read-only over `summary.json`, `report.json`, and `review-summary.json`

summary: Most underlying Node scripts remain runnable directly when a wrapper needs them without the binary
anchor: Most underlying Node scripts are still runnable directly

summary: Shipped runtime entrypoints like discover live-targets and evaluate live are Go-owned and should be called through the CLI
anchor: are Go-owned now and should be called through the CLI instead of direct

summary: Only the remaining wrapper and packet-builder utilities should be invoked directly
anchor: Only the remaining wrapper and packet-builder utilities below should be invoked directly
